reasoning: The stock price plunged sharply on July 31 following its Q2 earnings report, falling from around $293 down to $258 in early August. However, the drop appears to have been an unwarranted overreaction or tied to broader market volatility, as earnings actually beat expectations ($7.20 EPS vs $7.16 estimate) and revenues grew 11%. Analysts (e.g. at Wells Fargo) expressed surprise at the selloff, and the consensus price target remains around $379. Over the first half of August, the stock has shown intense positive momentum, recovering essentially all of its post-earnings losses to close at $292.11 on August 15. The statistical trend analysis from Yahoo Finance gives a combined probability of 0.56 for the near-term horizon based on this momentum and seasonal trends. I use 0.56 as my baseline for the 1-week forecast, and gracefully decay the probability toward 0.5 for the more distant horizons, per instructions.
